Re: Your Request for Advice

Dear Mr. scott:
You have requested confirmation of my tetephone advice
provided t9 you on behalf of Judge Harry iapadakis concerning the
carnpaign disclosure provisions of the political 
Reform Act
(Government Code Sections 81OOO-91015) .
Your letter, dated January 2, 1990, correctly summarizes my
advice that campaign funds raised prior to 1989 and deposited ii a
campaign bank account established for a previous elect-ion, may be
designated for a future election as 1ong as no contributions or
expenditures are made from the bank acc6unt in connection with the
previous election.
Enclosed are Forms 501 and 502. please complete these forms
to disclose the required infornation.
PLease do not hesitate to contact ne at (915) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

sections.conclusion: Your letter, dated January 2, 1990, correctly summarizes my advice that campaign funds raised prior to 1989 and deposited in a campaign bank account established for a previous election, may be designated for a future election as long as no contributions or expenditures are made from the bank account in connection with the previous election.
sections.question_synthetic: May campaign funds raised prior to 1989 and deposited in a campaign bank account established for a previous election be designated for a future election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign funds raised before 1989 and deposited in an existing campaign account may be redesignated for a future election, provided no contributions or expenditures are made from that account regarding the previous election.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal section headers for QUESTION and CONCLUSION but contains substantive advice content. Conclusion text paraphrases rather than formally labels as 'CONCLUSION' section. OCR quality issues present but content is recoverable. Advice was originally provided by telephone and confirmed in writing.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May campaign funds raised prior to 1989 and deposited in a campaign bank account established for a previous election be designated for a future election?

CONCLUSION: Your letter, dated January 2, 1990, correctly summarizes my advice that campaign funds raised prior to 1989 and deposited in a campaign bank account established for a previous election, may be designated for a future election as long as no contributions or expenditures are made from the bank account in connection with the previous election.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms advice regarding the reuse of prior campaign funds for a future election under the Political Reform Act. The letter confirms that funds raised before 1989 may be redesignated for a future election if no activity occurs in the account for the previous election.
